DiMeois a tertiary character onThe Sopranos. He is a criminal known to the Newark Police Department. This man committed one or several felonies in the past and was arrested by the Newark Police Department, with his mugshot being kept in their archives.[1]

characters.name: Aaron Arkaway
characters.description: Aaron Arkaway20062000InformationAliasesHoneyNationalityAmericanHomeNew JerseyGenderMaleHair ColorWhite (balding)Eye ColorBlueFateStatusAliveFamilyPartnerJanice Soprano(ex-girlfriend)Political InformationOccupation(s)MusicianBehind the ScenesFirst Appearance"He Is Risen"Final Appearance"The Fleshy Part of the Thigh"Portrayed ByTurk PipkinEpisode Count4 Aaron Arkawayis a recurring character inThe Sopranos. He is a devout evangelical Christian, amateur Christian rock songwriter, and the former boyfriend ofJanice Sopranowhen she was a born-again Christian. He is also narcoleptic, and falls asleep at inconvenient times, which causes many awkward interactions with the people he comes across. He is portrayed by actorTurk Pipkin. Janice Sopranobecame a born-again Christian after her encounter with Russian mobsters who assaulted her on orders fromSvetlana Kirilenko, her motherLivia's former nurse, after Janice stole Svetlana's prosthetic leg.[1]Some time later, she met Aaron and started dating him. The two wrote Christian rock songs together with the goal of eventually releasing and selling their album. Aaron is a staunch evangelical Christian, refusing to drink any type of alcohol whatsoever and rejectingTony's wine when he offered it to him duringCarmela's birthday party. However, Janice did continue to use cocaine while dating him,[7]and when they were composing songs together, a bottle of Carlo Rossi wine could be seen at the table, possibly meaning that it was Janice's, or that Aaron is a hypocrite to a certain extent, and still drinks when he wants to.[4]He commands a sense of awkwardness whenever he is around thefamily, and always greets people with the phrase "Have you heard the good news? He is risen." Aaron's branch of faith also seems to align with pro-life movements, as he was seen protesting at one such type of rally when he visited Tony at the hospital, although it's entirely possible that Aaron's faith and his views on the right to life aren't intertwined with one another. Apart from his religious views, he also seems like a gentle and kind person, going out of his way to visit Tony at the hospital and praying for his recovery.[6]

characters.name: Adriana La Cerva
characters.description: Adriana La CervaMainSeason 1Season 2Season 3Season 4Season 5InformationNicknamesAdeNationalityItalian-AmericanDate of BirthAugust 6, 1972HomeApartment,New Jersey,United StatesGenderFemaleHair ColorBlondeEye ColorBrownFateStatusDeceasedDate of deathNovember 30, 2004 (aged 32)Cause of DeathShot bySilvio DanteFamilyPartnerChristopher Moltisanti(fiancé)ParentsMr. La Cerva (father)Liz La Cerva(mother)Other RelativesJackie Aprile Sr.(uncle)Richie Aprile(uncle)Rosalie Aprile(aunt through marriage)Jackie Aprile Jr.(cousin)Kelli Aprile(cousin)Richard Aprile, Jr.(cousin)Vito Spatafore(cousin)Bryan Spatafore(cousin)Vito Spatafore Jr.(first cousin once removed)Francesca Spatafore(first cousin once removed)Marie Spatafore(cousin-in-law)Unnamed cousinUnnamed auntPolitical InformationOccupation(s)Nightclub OwnerHostessAffiliationCrazy HorseNuovo VesuvioBehind the ScenesFirst Appearance"46 Long"Final Appearance"Long Term Parking" (alive)"Cold Stones" (dream)Portrayed ByDrea de MatteoEpisode Count47 Adriana La Cervais a major character onThe Sopranos. She is the girlfriend ofChristopher Moltisantiand a classic mob moll, loving him and the various privileges coming from his position in theDiMeo family, while also possessing an interest in club and music management. She is portrayed byDrea de Matteo. Adriana with her beloved boyfriendChristopher Moltisanti.

characters.name: Agent Malatesta
characters.description: Agent MalatestaInformationNationalityItalian-AmericanGenderFemaleHair ColorBrownEye ColorBrownFateStatusAlivePolitical InformationOccupation(s)FBI special agentAffiliationFBIBehind the ScenesFirst AppearanceMr. Ruggerio's NeighborhoodFinal AppearanceMr. Ruggerio's NeighborhoodPortrayed ByColleen WerthmannEpisode Count1Agent Malatestais a supporting character portrayed by Colleen Werthmann. She is a special agent with theFBIand part of the team trying to arrest and prosecuteDiMeo crime familystreet bossTony Soprano. She is first seen in the meeting room withFrank Cubitoso,Dwight Harrisand several otherFBIagents. The room determines that informant andDiMeosoldierSalvatore Bonpensierois no longer relaying credible information and the agents decide to "bin" him. Next, the FBI plan to bug DiMeo street bossTony Soprano's house.Skip Liparitells the other agents that Tony tries not to speak with his criminal cohorts inside the house, but if he has to, he will talk in the basement because of the noisy AC ducts that are installed and he feels safe from listening devices. The FBI team hatch a plan to bug the basement while the family is out.

characters.name: Ahmed
characters.description: Ahmed al-NajafiInformationFull NameAhmed al-Najafi (أحمد النجفي)NationalityUnknownHomeBrooklyn, New York, United StatesGenderMaleHair ColorBlackEye ColorBrownFateStatusAliveBehind the ScenesFirst Appearance"Join the Club"Final Appearance"Chasing It"Portrayed ByTaleb AdlahEpisode Count5 Ahmed al-Najafi(played byTaleb Adlah) is a Muslim associate ofChristopher "Chris" Moltisanti. He always appears with his friendMuhammad. Muhammad and Ahmed, together known as "The Arabs" to most, live inBrooklyn, New Yorkand were involved in Christopher andJames "Murmur" Zancone's credit card scam. Specifically, they are the buyers of the card numbers, which Muhammad and Ahmed use for fraudulent use online without being traced. They are frequent customers at theBada-Bing, but according to Tony, they had disappeared for a while before resuming their visits, at which point they began to regularly mingle with men in "head gear and the beard, the whole fundamental bit". In their first speaking roles, Ahmed and Muhammad meet with Christopher to buy credit cards taken in the names of customers to variousDiMeo familyfronts and ask him about acquiring TEC-9 semi-automatic pistols with extended magazines for a "family situation" involving Muhammad's former brother-in-law. They also gave well wishes to Tony's recent discharge from the hospital and handed over some dates as a gift.

characters.name: Al Lombardo
characters.description: Alphonse LombardoInformationFull NameAlphonse LombardoNicknamesAlNationalityItalian-AmericanGenderMaleHair ColorBrown (balding)Eye ColorBrownFateStatusAliveFamilySpouseRita Lombardo(wife)ChildrenKelli Lombardo Moltisanti(daughter)Other RelativesCaitlyn Moltisanti(granddaughter)Christopher Moltisanti(son-in-law)Political InformationOccupation(s)Hardware store cashierBehind the ScenesFirst Appearance"Walk Like a Man"Final Appearance"Kennedy and Heidi"Portrayed ByDennis PaladinoEpisode Count2Al Lombardois the father ofKelli Lombardo Moltisanti. Al works at a hardware store. One day, there’s a line of off duty cops at his store who want to share in the profitable contraband. Al introduces his son-in-lawChristopher Moltisantito one of the cops. One night, he catchesPaulie Germaniand an associate trying to steal tools from his store and young Paulie shuts down Al’s protest by claiming to have left a message with Chris asking for tools and tells him not to make a problem where there isn’t one.("Walk Like a Man")Al attends his Christopher’s funeral and expresses frustration with the death of his son-in-law leaving his daughter widowed and his granddaughter fatherless.("Kennedy and Heidi")

characters.name: Alan Sapinsly
characters.description: Alan SapinslyInformationNationalityAmericanHomeSea Bright, New JerseyGenderMaleHair ColorBrownEye ColorBrownFateStatusAliveFamilySpousePatricia Reingold-Sapinsly(wife)Political InformationOccupation(s)LawyerBehind the ScenesFirst AppearanceWhitecapsFinal AppearanceWhitecapsPortrayed ByBruce AltmanEpisode Count1 Alan Sapinslyis a house attorney who ownsWhitecaps. When Alan first meetsTony Sopranohe’s offered cash in the shortest possible time the law allows. When Alan calls the current buyer ofWhitecaps Housethe buyer is having difficulty with getting a home loan so Alan negotiates but he also threatens his way out of the contract. One night when Tony stayed overnight at theWhitecaps Househe told Alan that he doesn’t wanna buy it anymore, but Alan declined so he could release Tony from the contract. Alan calls Tony about releasing him from the sale and he’d keep the $200,000 deposit. He offers to negotiate but Tony declines. One  day Alan and his wife, Patricia have friends over for lunch and they get interrupted by a very loud Dean Martin concert in a boat out in the harbor. Patricia wonders what the boat is and Alan looks at it through his binoculars and reads “The Stugots.” They try to ignore the noise by closing the patio doors. Later that night, Alan and Patricia relax outside on the patio and suddenly the music out on the boat starts up again and Alan isn’t happy about it. He says call the police but Patricia says they’ll just turn it down again when the police arrive. Patricia angrily tells Alan he should just give the money to Tony.("Whitecaps") When Alan first meetsTony Sopranohe’s offered cash in the shortest possible time the law allows. When Alan calls the current buyer ofWhitecaps Housethe buyer is having difficulty with getting a home loan so Alan negotiates but he also threatens his way out of the contract. He says call the police but Patricia says they’ll just turn it down again when the police arrive. Patricia angrily tells Alan he should just give the money to Tony.("Whitecaps")

One  day Alan and his wife, Patricia have friends over for lunch and they get interrupted by a very loud Dean Martin concert in a boat out in the harbor. Patricia wonders what the boat is and Alan looks at it through his binoculars and reads “The Stugots.” They try to ignore the noise by closing the patio doors. Later that night, Alan and Patricia relax outside on the patio and suddenly the music out on the boat starts up again and Alan isn’t happy about it. He says call the police but Patricia says they’ll just turn it down again when the police arrive. Patricia angrily tells Alan he should just give the money to Tony.("Whitecaps") When Alan first meetsTony Sopranohe’s offered cash in the shortest possible time the law allows. When Alan calls the current buyer ofWhitecaps Housethe buyer is having difficulty with getting a home loan so Alan negotiates but he also threatens his way out of the contract. One night when Tony stayed overnight at theWhitecaps Househe told Alan that he doesn’t wanna buy it anymore, but Alan declined so he could release Tony from the contract. He says call the police but Patricia says they’ll just turn it down again when the police arrive. Patricia angrily tells Alan he should just give the money to Tony.("Whitecaps")

characters.name: Albert Barese
characters.description: Albert BareseInformationAliasesAlly BoyNationalityItalian-AmericanDate of BirthSeptember 1962HomeJersey City,New Jersey,United StatesGenderMaleHair ColorBlackEye ColorBlueFateStatusAliveFamilyOther RelativesLarry Barese(cousin)Larry Barese's Wife(cousin-in-law)Melissa Barese(first cousin once removed)Unnamed auntUnnamed first cousin once-removed by marriagePolitical InformationOccupation(s)MobsterAffiliationDiMeo/Soprano crime familyBarese CrewRankSoldier(pre-1999–1999, 2003–present)ActingCapo(1999–2003)Behind the ScenesFirst Appearance"The Knight in White Satin Armor"Final Appearance"The Strong, Silent Type"Also Appears In"Rat Pack"Portrayed ByRichard MaldoneEpisode Count10 Albert"Ally Boy"Bareseis a supporting character onThe Sopranos. He is actingcapoof theBarese Crewfor his cousinLorenzo "Larry Boy" Baresein theDiMeo/Soprano crime family, and is known for his short-sighted nature and tendency to repeat other people's words. He is portrayed byRichard Maldone. He is a paternal cousin of theDiMeo familycapo Larry Barese. Albert was made acting capo of theBarese crewwhen Larry was sent to jail awaiting trial for racketeering.

characters.name: Albie Cianflone
characters.description: Albie CianfloneInformationFull NameAlbert CianfloneAliasesAlbieAlNationalityItalian-AmericanDate of BirthJune 12, 1940HomeBrooklyn, New York,United StatesGenderMaleHair ColorBlack (greying, receding)Eye ColorBrownFateStatusAlivePolitical InformationOccupation(s)MobsterAffiliationLupertazzi crime familyRankConsigliere(2004–present)Behind the ScenesFirst Appearance"The Fleshy Part of the Thigh"Final Appearance"Made in America"Portrayed ByJohn "Cha Cha" CiarciaEpisode Count13 Albert"Albie"Cianfloneis a supporting character onThe Sopranos. He is theconsiglieretoPhil Leotardoin theLupertazzi crime family, and often arranges business transactions with theDiMeo crime family. He is portrayed byJohn "Cha Cha" Ciarcia. Albie calmsPhildown after he starts ranting aboutVito. Out of all theLupertazzis, perhaps excludingGerry Torciano, Albie seems to be the most level-headed and cautious one. When gathering with the othercaposto discuss whether or not it was whoTonyblew up the wire room and what they should do about him, Albie is the one who talks the least. Then, whenPhildecides to have Tony, and the other top members of theDiMeo crime familykilled, Albie says that he "agree[s] in spirit", but that he doesn't know if he's ready to put that plan into motion. Albie did seem to go along with a plan if a final verdict had been given, however, and seemed to adopt more of a mob mentality most of the time. Like most other mobsters, Albie was a homophobe, as he demonstrated when he too was outraged at Tony "harboring a f*****", as Phil put it (referring toVito Spatafore).

characters.name: Alec Kastropovich
characters.description: Alec KastropovichInformationFull NameAleksandr KastropovichNicknamesAlecNationalityUkrainian-AmericanDate of Birth1944HomeQueens, New YorkGenderMaleHair ColorWhiteEye ColorBlueFateStatusDeceasedDate of deathNovember, 2007Cause of DeathShot in the head byItaloFamilyChildrenYaryna Kastropovich(daughter)Behind the ScenesFirst AppearanceThe Blue CometFinal AppearanceThe Blue CometPortrayed ByAleks ShaklinEpisode Count1 Alec Kastropovich(played by Aleks Shakiln) is a minor character onThe Sopranos. One night, Alec hears a knock on his door and he answers and it’sItalo, who mistakes him forPhil Leotardoand ends up killing him by shooting him in the head once and then twice when his dead body fells down. After he’s shot, hisscared daughterrushes downstairs and ends up being shot and killed as well.("The Blue Comet")

characters.name: Alex Mahaffey
characters.description: Alex MahaffeyInformationFull NameAlexander MahaffeyAliasesAlexNationalityAmericanGenderMaleHair ColorDirty Blond(Balding)Eye ColorBrownFateStatusAliveFamilyPartnerHis loverPolitical InformationOccupation(s)GamblerAffiliationDiMeo crime familyRankAssociateBehind the ScenesFirst AppearancePilotFinal AppearancePilotPortrayed ByMichael GastonEpisode Count1Alex Mahaffeyis a supporting character portrayed byMichael Gaston. He is an associate of theDiMeo crime family. Mahaffey is a gambling addict who has borrowed money from DiMeo capoTony Sopranoand loansharkHesh Rabkinto fuel his habit. He works for US/HMO which is a medical insurance company. Soprano and then DiMeo associateChristopher Moltisantispot Mahaffey outside the offices of US/HMO walking with a woman who Christopher describes as "the boo-boo in pink." Alex spots the two mobsters and flees, kicking Christopher to get away. Soprano pursues Mahaffey in Moltisanti's car and knocks him down. He breaks his leg but Tony Soprano nevertheless continues to beat him up and demands payment. Soprano later sends DiMeo soldierSalvatore BonpensieroandHesh Rabkinto force Mahaffey into funding fraudulent medical claims, which was based on Tony's earlier experience with a genuine medical claim of an MRI. Tony's proposal is to clear Mahaffey's debt by submitting multiple claims with his company. The two men assure Mahaffey that their bogus claims are clever counterfeits. When Mahaffey is unsure of this, Hesh offers to give him a view of the waterfalls. Just then Bonpensiero, who had finished an ice cream cone, litters the wrapper into the chasm. Realizing that would be his final fate should he not comply, Mahaffey begins planning the process with Rabkin, making threat of the deadly plunge unnecessary.

Alfred lives in a house on Atwell Avenue in Providence, Rhode Island withFrank CrisciandLou Galina. Together they acted as hitmen for different mob crews and were known as theAtwell Avenue Boys.TonysendsChristopher, andSilviocome to visit Alfred, Frank and Lou after being recommended byJunior Sopranoto put a hit onJohnny Sack. When Lou tells a story of how he and Frank murdered a man named Tommy. Alfred says that they could arrange a hit on Johnny in the same way, but Silvio says it doesn't need to be done that way.("The Weight")

characters.name: Ally Vandermeed
characters.description: Ally Vandermeedis one ofMeadow Soprano’s close friends. Ally VandermeedInformationFull NameAlexandra VandermeedNicknamesAllyNationalityAmericanGenderFemaleHair ColorDirty BlondEye ColorBrownFateStatusAliveBehind the ScenesFirst Appearance"Boca"Final Appearance"Boca"Portrayed ByCara JedellEpisode Count1 Ally and Meadow are on the same soccer team. It’s revealed that theCoach Don Hauserhas engaging in an inappropriate sexual relationship with Ally, which is ongoing. When Ally learns that her coach is moving on, she attempts suicide by slitting her wrists while her friends are drinking in the park. It becomes known that the coach was sexually abusing Ally. When Meadow’s parents learn about the suicide attempt, she tells them about the inappropriate relationship between Ally and Don Hauser, resulting in her receiving justice and closure when Hauser is arrested for his crimes.

When Meadow’s parents learn about the suicide attempt, she tells them about the inappropriate relationship between Ally and Don Hauser, resulting in her receiving justice and closure when Hauser is arrested for his crimes.

characters.description: Alyssa GiglioneSeason 2Season 5Season 6InformationFull NameAlyssa GiglioneNationalityItalian-AmericanDate of Birth1996HomeBrewster, New YorkGenderFemaleHair ColorBrownEye ColorBrownFateStatusAliveFamilyParentsBarbara Giglione(mother)Thomas Giglione Jr.(father)SiblingsThomas Giglione III(brother)Other RelativesLivia Soprano(maternal grandmother)Johnny Soprano(maternal grandfather)Junior Soprano(great-uncle)Ercole Soprano(great-uncle)Tony Soprano(uncle)Janice Soprano(aunt)Meadow Soprano(cousin)Anthony Soprano, Jr.(cousin)Domenica Baccalieri(cousin)Carmela Soprano(aunt by marriage)Bobby Baccalieri(uncle by marriage)Harpo Soprano(cousin)Thomas Giglione Sr.(paternal grandfather)Quintina Blundetto(great-aunt)Tony Blundetto(first cousin once-removed)Kelly Blundetto(second cousin)Jason and Justin Blundetto(second cousins)Corrado Soprano, Sr.(great-grandfather)Mariangela Soprano(great-grandmother)Bobby Baccalieri III(step-cousin)Sophia Baccalieri(step-cousin)Behind the ScenesFirst AppearanceGuy Walks into a Psychiatrist's Office...Final AppearanceWalk Like a ManPortrayed ByUnknown actress (Seasons 2 and 3)Madison Connolly (seasons 5 and 6)Episode Count4Alyssa Giglioneis the daughter ofBarbaraandThomas Giglione Jr.and the niece ofTony Soprano. Alyssa is first seen the the episode "Guy Walks into Phychiatrist’s Office...", when the family arrives at the Soprano’s house. She appears next in the episode "Proshai, Livushka", when her family attends hergrandmother’sfuneral.
